终于有个不用侧边栏的 Agent App 设计，但是像浏览器一样顶部 Tab 的体验是否好还不好说。

有个问题就是内存占用必然会更厉害，因为侧边栏切换，默认全局只有一个对话打开，切换了之前的就可以销毁了。顶部 Tab 切换，默认对话都要会保留状态，这样会话一多内存就会占用厉害。

Google cooked with the features but not the models

Google I/O 2026 just satisfyingly answered every question about where Gemini is heading.

New models. A personal AI agent. Compute-based pricing. Background agents that monitor the web for you.

Here's everything that matters for AI operators: https://t.co/4ysjTzHe4y

Gemini 3.5 Flash is the new default model.

It beats 3.1 Pro across coding, agentic, and multimodal benchmarks while running 4x faster than other frontier models in output tokens per second.

Available now in the Gemini app, Search, Antigravity 2.0, and the API.

3.5 Pro is in https://t.co/rhtlhDWgr5

Gemini Omni is Google's new creation model.

It accepts image, audio, video, and text as input and outputs video grounded in real-world knowledge.

You can edit the output using natural conversational language. Not timeline editing. Not keyframing. Talking to it.

Google Flow and

Gemini Spark is Google's answer to the "personal AI agent" question.

It integrates with Gmail, Docs, and Workspace apps. Takes actions on your behalf. Third-party tool support via MCP coming this summer.

Available next week for AI Ultra subscribers ($100/mo).

Daily Brief is https://t.co/9v0jGWyvT3

Google is replacing daily prompt limits with a compute-based usage model.

Simple text prompts use less compute. Complex video or coding prompts use more. Limits refresh every five hours until your weekly cap.

This is a better model for power users who mix light and heavy tasks. https://t.co/oi53ZuqUXs

This is the one that should get your attention.

Google is launching information agents that work in the background 24/7.

You set a question. The agent monitors blogs, news sites, social posts, and real-time data across finance, shopping, and sports. It alerts you when something

Gmail and Docs are getting voice-driven AI modes.

Gmail Live lets you search and interact with your emails conversationally on mobile.

Docs Live lets you create and edit documents by talking to them.

Google Keep gets the same treatment. Speak your thoughts, and it organizes

The pattern across every announcement: Google is moving from AI that answers to AI that acts.

Agents that monitor. Agents that shop. Agents that manage your inbox. A compute model that charges for what you use, not how many times you click send.

demo 2 展示的是一个更现实的人机协作流程。

人负责在 Open Design 里表达需求和判断方向，Codex 负责在同一个 workspace 里检查产物、打开页面、测试交互、指出问题，并把反馈推进到下一轮修改。

设计不再是一次性生成，而是人 + agent 在同一套环境里一起迭代。

冷知识：

很多人以为 GEO是一种很技术的活，

其实实际上和技术没关系，就像 gemini 和 openai 基本都是拿 reddit 的数据来做训练，

现在白帽的方式，是可以把你想要吹嘘的公司（以及对应介绍） ，塞到大模型后训练数据进行训练（日更）就好。

大模型自动记住你的公司。

开机的时候，让 AI 往里边推一个 To-do、日历，一些基础的需要记的信息

用了几天 Mac 版豆包输入法，已经离不开了。

准确性、中英文混合输入、专业名词的识别，响应速度，都是市面上最顶的。

我现在手机上已经离不开豆包输入法了，一直在等待桌面端上线。前几天发现 MacOS 版终于来了，赶紧下载试了一下。

我觉得表现最好的就是多语言混合输入。 https://t.co/ztZJFRyANl

Short dramas have quietly become a massive entertainment format.

Think next-gen soap operas: serialized, mobile-first, and monetized like games.

In China, they already generate more revenue than the domestic box office.

And now AI is going to blow this format wide open 👇 https://t.co/zwsuXn1cxP

Short dramas are almost tailor-made for AI.

They typically feature small casts with limited sets, constraining the "world" in which you have to generate videos.

And audiences care about the hook, pacing, and cliffhanger - they don't expect HBO-level production quality. https://t.co/TEnGiqMhL1

There are a few layers of the stack:

1) Foundation models that generate videos (e.g. Seedance, Kling)

2) Creation tools & agents that guide generations + organize production (e.g. Martini, Preview)

3) Distribution platforms for reaching users (e.g. ReelShort, DramaBox)

There are opportunities for AI to disrupt every layer.

We’re already seeing startups emerge across the stack - from creator tools like @plotpartyai to AI-native distribution platforms like TrueShort and @watchmochi.

AI makes content faster + cheaper to produce. And it also https://t.co/0fkrGTUMwY

@plotpartyai @watchmochi @techreview If you’re building something here - whether it’s models, creator tools, or AI-native entertainment platforms - I’d love to hear from you!
